dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (lah on obligation-statements)
notes: Sentence-final 'lah' reinforces an obligation/norm ('memang harus' = 'of course must / really have to'). 'Memang' already adds emphasis-of-fact; 'lah' compounds it. The translation 'Of course we have to, obviously' is slightly redundant in English (both 'of course' and 'obviously') — that's because Indonesian uses two intensifiers ('memang' + 'lah') where English typically uses one. This is the kind of fixture that will need real-conversation calibration. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: the doubling might be wrong — could be 'We obviously have to' (single intensifier) or 'Of course we have to!' (exclamation instead of secondary intensifier).
